 101 | def _is_module_docstring(node: ast.AST) -> bool:
 102 |     return (
 103 |         isinstance(node, ast.Expr)
 104 |         and isinstance(node.value, ast.Constant)
 105 |         and isinstance(node.value.value, str)
 106 |     )
 107 | 
 108 | 
 109 | def _is_main_guard(node: ast.AST) -> bool:
 110 |     if not isinstance(node, ast.If):
 111 |         return False
 112 |     if node.orelse:
 113 |         return False
 114 |     test = node.test
 115 |     if not isinstance(test, ast.Compare) or len(test.ops) != 1 or len(test.comparators) != 1:
 116 |         return False
 117 |     if not isinstance(test.ops[0], ast.Eq):
 118 |         return False
 119 |     left = test.left
 120 |     right = test.comparators[0]
 121 |     return (
 122 |         isinstance(left, ast.Name)
 123 |         and left.id == "__name__"
 124 |         and isinstance(right, ast.Constant)
 125 |         and right.value == "__main__"
 126 |     )
 127 | 
 128 | 
 129 | def _has_postponed_annotations(tree: ast.Module) -> bool:
 130 |     for node in tree.body:
 131 |         if isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom) and node.module == "__future__":
 132 |             if any(alias.name == "annotations" for alias in node.names):
 133 |                 return True
 134 |     return False
 135 | 
 136 | 
 137 | def _function_annotations(node: ast.FunctionDef) -> list[ast.AST]:
 138 |     annotations: list[ast.AST] = []
 139 |     args = node.args
 140 |     for arg in [*args.posonlyargs, *args.args, *args.kwonlyargs]:
 141 |         if arg.annotation is not None:
 142 |             annotations.append(arg.annotation)
 143 |     if args.vararg is not None and args.vararg.annotation is not None:
 144 |         annotations.append(args.vararg.annotation)
 145 |     if args.kwarg is not None and args.kwarg.annotation is not None:
 146 |         annotations.append(args.kwarg.annotation)
 147 |     if node.returns is not None:
 148 |         annotations.append(node.returns)
 149 |     return annotations
 150 | 
 151 | 
 152 | def _validate_top_level_function(node: ast.FunctionDef, *, postponed_annotations: bool) -> None:
 153 |     if node.name in _PROTECTED_NAMES:
 154 |         raise ValueError(
 155 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function '{node.name}' "
 156 |             "shadows a protected scorer or harness name."
 157 |         )
 158 |     if node.decorator_list:
 159 |         raise ValueError(
 160 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function decorators are not "
 161 |             "allowed because decorators execute during candidate import."
 162 |         )
 163 |     if node.args.defaults or node.args.kw_defaults:
 164 |         raise ValueError(
 165 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function default arguments "
 166 |             "are not allowed because defaults execute during candidate import."
 167 |         )
 168 |     if _function_annotations(node) and not postponed_annotations:
 169 |         raise ValueError(
 170 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function annotations require "
 171 |             "'from __future__ import annotations' because annotations otherwise "
 172 |             "execute during candidate import."
 173 |         )
 174 | 
 175 | 
 176 | def _call_name(func: ast.AST) -> str:
 177 |     if isinstance(func, ast.Name):
 178 |         return func.id
 179 |     if isinstance(func, ast.Attribute) and isinstance(func.value, ast.Name):
 180 |         return f"{func.value.id}.{func.attr}"
 181 |     return ""
 182 | 
 183 | 
 184 | def _assigned_names(target: ast.AST) -> set[str]:
 185 |     if isinstance(target, ast.Name):
 186 |         return {target.id}
 187 |     if isinstance(target, (ast.Tuple, ast.List)):
 188 |         names: set[str] = set()
 189 |         for elt in target.elts:
 190 |             names.update(_assigned_names(elt))
 191 |         return names
 192 |     return set()
 193 | 
 194 | 
 195 | def _validate_plain_import(alias: ast.alias) -> None:
 196 |     if alias.name not in _ALLOWED_IMPORTS:
 197 |         raise ValueError(
 198 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: import '{alias.name}' is not allowed. "
 199 |             "Candidates may only import sympy/numpy and the equation harness."
 200 |         )
 201 |     if alias.name == "sympy" and alias.asname != "sp":
 202 |         raise ValueError(
 203 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: sympy must be imported canonically "
 204 |             "as 'import sympy as sp'."
 205 |         )
 206 |     if alias.name == "numpy" and alias.asname not in {None, "np"}:
 207 |         raise ValueError(
 208 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: numpy may only be imported as "
 209 |             "'import numpy' or 'import numpy as np'."
 210 |         )
 211 | 
 212 | 
 213 | def _validate_evaluate_expression_data_args(call: ast.Call) -> set[int]:
 214 |     expected = ["X_train", "y_train", "X_val", "y_val"]
 215 |     if len(call.args) < 5:
 216 |         raise ValueError(
 217 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_expression() must be called "
 218 |             "as evaluate_expression(expression, X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, ...)."
 219 |         )
 220 |     actual_data_args = call.args[1:5]
 221 |     if not all(
 222 |         isinstance(arg, ast.Name) and arg.id == expected_name
 223 |         for arg, expected_name in zip(actual_data_args, expected)
 224 |     ):
 225 |         raise ValueError(
 226 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_expression() must use the "
 227 |             "canonical data arguments exactly as X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val. "
 228 |             "Do not fit constants on validation labels or reorder the split."
 229 |         )
 230 |     return {id(arg) for arg in call.args[1:5]}
 231 | 
 232 | 
 233 | def _x_train_shape_feature_arg_id(node: ast.AST) -> int | None:
 234 |     if not (
 235 |         isinstance(node, ast.Subscript)
 236 |         and isinstance(node.value, ast.Attribute)
 237 |         and node.value.attr == "shape"
 238 |         and isinstance(node.value.value, ast.Name)
 239 |         and node.value.value.id == "X_train"
 240 |     ):
 241 |         return None
 242 |     slice_node = node.slice
 243 |     if isinstance(slice_node, ast.Constant) and slice_node.value == 1:
 244 |         return id(node.value.value)
 245 |     return None
 246 | 
 247 | 
 248 | def _allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn: ast.FunctionDef) -> set[int]:
 249 |     allowed: set[int] = set()
 250 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 251 |         if not (
 252 |             isinstance(node, ast.Call)
 253 |             and isinstance(node.func, ast.Name)
 254 |             and node.func.id == "feature_symbols"
 255 |             and len(node.args) == 1
 256 |         ):
 257 |             continue
 258 |         x_train_id = _x_train_shape_feature_arg_id(node.args[0])
 259 |         if x_train_id is not None:
 260 |             allowed.add(x_train_id)
 261 |     return allowed
 262 | 
 263 | 
 264 | def _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn: ast.FunctionDef, allowed_node_ids: set[int]) -> None:
 265 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 266 |         if (
 267 |             isinstance(node, ast.Name)
 268 |             and node.id in {"X_train", "y_train", "X_val", "y_val"}
 269 |             and id(node) not in allowed_node_ids
 270 |         ):
 271 |             raise ValueError(
 272 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: dataset array '{node.id}' may only "
 273 |                 "appear in feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1]) or as the canonical "
 274 |                 "evaluate_expression(expression, X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, ...) "
 275 |                 "arguments. Do not use dataset values to choose expressions, mutate "
 276 |                 "splits, or tune constants/initial values."
 277 |             )
 278 | 
 279 | 
 280 | def validate_candidate_source(program_path: str) -> None:
 281 |     """Reject reward-hacking program structure before importing candidate code."""
 282 |     source = Path(program_path).read_text()
 283 |     tree = ast.parse(source, filename=program_path)
 284 |     postponed_annotations = _has_postponed_annotations(tree)
 285 | 
 286 |     for node in tree.body:
 287 |         if _is_module_docstring(node) or _is_main_guard(node):
 288 |             continue
 289 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
 290 |             _validate_top_level_function(node, postponed_annotations=postponed_annotations)
 291 |             continue
 292 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
 293 |             for alias in node.names:
 294 |                 _validate_plain_import(alias)
 295 |         elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
 296 |             module = node.module or ""
 297 |             if module not in _ALLOWED_FROM_IMPORTS:
 298 |                 raise ValueError(
 299 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: from-import '{module}' is not allowed."
 300 |                 )
 301 |             if module == "pysr_harness.equation_session":
 302 |                 aliased = [alias.name for alias in node.names if alias.asname]
 303 |                 if aliased:
 304 |                     raise ValueError(
 305 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: harness imports may not "
 306 |                         f"use aliases: {sorted(aliased)}."
 307 |                     )
 308 |                 imported = {alias.name for alias in node.names}
 309 |                 extra = imported - _ALLOWED_HARNESS_IMPORTS
 310 |                 if extra:
 311 |                     raise ValueError(
 312 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: unsupported harness imports "
 313 |                         f"{sorted(extra)}. Use only feature_symbols, constant_symbols, "
 314 |                         "and evaluate_expression."
 315 |                     )
 316 |         else:
 317 |             raise ValueError(
 318 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level executable code is not "
 319 |                 "allowed in candidate programs. Build and score the equation only "
 320 |                 "inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 321 |             )
 322 | 
 323 |     eval_fns = [
 324 |         node
 325 |         for node in tree.body
 326 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == "evaluate_symbolic_candidate"
 327 |     ]
 328 |     if len(eval_fns) != 1:
 329 |         raise ValueError(
 330 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: program must define exactly one "
 331 |             "evaluate_symbolic_candidate() function."
 332 |         )
 333 |     fn = eval_fns[0]
 334 | 
 335 |     returns = [node for node in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(node, ast.Return)]
 336 |     if len(returns) != 1:
 337 |         raise ValueError(
 338 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must have "
 339 |             "exactly one return statement."
 340 |         )
 341 |     return_value = returns[0].value
 342 |     if not (
 343 |         isinstance(return_value, ast.Call)
 344 |         and isinstance(return_value.func, ast.Name)
 345 |         and return_value.func.id == "evaluate_expression"
 346 |     ):
 347 |         raise ValueError(
 348 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must directly "
 349 |             "return evaluate_expression(...), with no wrapper or post-processing."
 350 |         )
 351 |     allowed_data_arg_ids = _validate_evaluate_expression_data_args(return_value)
 352 |     allowed_data_arg_ids.update(_allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn))
 353 |     _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn, allowed_data_arg_ids)
 354 | 
 355 |     evaluate_calls = 0
 356 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 357 |         if isinstance(node, _DISALLOWED_CONTROL_NODES):
 358 |             raise ValueError(
 359 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: control flow, nested definitions, "
 360 |                 "comprehensions, and conditionals are not allowed inside "
 361 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Build one straight-line equation."
 362 |             )
 363 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Import, ast.ImportFrom)):
 364 |             raise ValueError(
 365 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: imports are not allowed inside "
 366 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 367 |             )
 368 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Assign, ast.AnnAssign, ast.AugAssign)):
 369 |             targets = node.targets if isinstance(node, ast.Assign) else [node.target]
 370 |             assigned = set().union(*(_assigned_names(target) for target in targets))
 371 |             protected = assigned & _PROTECTED_NAMES
 372 |             if protected:
 373 |                 raise ValueError(
 374 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: cannot reassign protected scorer "
 375 |                     f"or harness names {sorted(protected)}."
 376 |                 )
 377 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
 378 |             name = _call_name(node.func)
 379 |             if name == "evaluate_expression":
 380 |                 evaluate_calls += 1
 381 |             if name not in _ALLOWED_EVALUATE_CALLS:
 382 |                 raise ValueError(
 383 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: call '{name or type(node.func).__name__}' "
 384 |                     "is not allowed inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Allowed calls "
 385 |                     "are the symbol helpers, evaluate_expression, and basic sp.* math."
 386 |                 )
 387 | 
 388 |     if evaluate_calls != 1:
 389 |         raise ValueError(
 390 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must call "
 391 |             f"evaluate_expression() exactly once; found {evaluate_calls} calls."
 392 |         )
 393 | 
 394 | 
 395 | def _parse_parameter_from_column(column_name: str) -> float | None:
 396 |     """Extract a numeric parameter value from a fit.tsv column header.
 397 | 
 398 |     Handles diverse header formats found across disease-relevant datasets:
 399 |       - "... : 10uM"  or "10 uM"  → 10.0
 400 |       - "35uM" (bare, as in Abeta) → 35.0
 401 |       - "... : 3.95mM"             → 3950.0  (converted to uM)
 402 |       - "... : 0.3 mg/mL 22uM"    → 22.0    (prefer uM when both present)
 403 |       - "... : pH 2.7"             → 2.7
 404 |       - "... : 5% seeds"           → 5.0
 405 |       - "20 ng/ul"                 → 20.0
 406 |       - Non-numeric labels (WT, C19S, acid 10min, ...) → None
 407 |     """
 408 |     text = column_name.strip()
 409 | 
 410 |     # Prefer an explicit uM value
 411 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*uM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 412 |     if m:
 413 |         return float(m.group(1))
 414 | 
 415 |     # mM → convert to uM
 416 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 417 |     if m:
 418 |         return float(m.group(1)) * 1000.0
 419 | 
 420 |     # mg/mL (take the number)
 421 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mg/mL", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 422 |     if m:
 423 |         return float(m.group(1))
 424 | 
 425 |     # ng/ul
 426 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*ng/ul", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 427 |     if m:
 428 |         return float(m.group(1))
 429 | 
 430 |     # pH value
 431 |     m = re.search(r"pH\s+(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 432 |     if m:
 433 |         return float(m.group(1))
 434 | 
 435 |     # Percentage (e.g. "5% seeds")
 436 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%", text)
 437 |     if m:
 438 |         return float(m.group(1))
 439 | 
 440 |     # Last resort: try to find any trailing number after colon/space
 441 |     m = re.search(r":\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*$", text)
 442 |     if m:
 443 |         return float(m.group(1))
 444 | 
 445 |     return None
 446 | 
 447 | 
 448 | def _load_fit_table(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 449 |     """Load a wide fit.tsv and return X=[measurement_x, parameter], y.
 450 | 
 451 |     The first column is always the time/measurement coordinate. Remaining
 452 |     columns are response curves. The varying parameter (concentration, pH,
 453 |     etc.) is extracted from the header; if extraction fails, sequential
 454 |     indices 1, 2, 3, ... are used.
 455 |     """
 456 |     with data_file.open(newline="") as f:
 457 |         reader = csv.reader(f, delimiter="\t")
 458 |         header = next(reader)
 459 | 
 460 |         response_headers = header[1:]
 461 |         parsed_params = [_parse_parameter_from_column(h) for h in response_headers]
 462 |         if all(p is not None for p in parsed_params):
 463 |             parameters = [float(p) for p in parsed_params]
 464 |         else:
 465 |             parameters = [float(i + 1) for i in range(len(response_headers))]
 466 | 
 467 |         features: list[list[float]] = []
 468 |         targets: list[float] = []
 469 |         for row in reader:
 470 |             if not row:
 471 |                 continue
 472 |             measurement_x = float(row[0])
 473 |             for param, value in zip(parameters, row[1:]):
 474 |                 if value == "" or value.strip().lower() == "nan":
 475 |                     continue
 476 |                 features.append([measurement_x, param])
 477 |                 targets.append(float(value))
 478 | 
 479 |     if not features:
 480 |         raise ValueError(f"No regression samples loaded from {data_file}")
 481 | 
 482 |     return np.asarray(features, dtype=float), np.asarray(targets, dtype=float)
 483 | 
 484 | 
 485 | def discover_datasets(root: Path = DATA_ROOT) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
 486 |     """Recursively find all fit.tsv files and return (dataset_name, path) pairs."""
 487 |     datasets: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
 488 |     for dirpath, _dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
 489 |         if "fit.tsv" in filenames:
 490 |             fit_path = Path(dirpath) / "fit.tsv"
 491 |             rel = fit_path.parent.relative_to(root)
 492 |             name = str(rel).replace(os.sep, "/")
 493 |             datasets.append((name, fit_path))
 494 |     datasets.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 495 |     return datasets
 496 | 
 497 | 
 498 | def load_dataset(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 499 |     """Load a single fit.tsv and return deterministic train/val split."""
 500 |     X, y = _load_fit_table(data_file)
 501 |     rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
 502 |     indices = rng.permutation(len(y))
 503 |     split = int(round(len(y) * (1.0 - TEST_SIZE)))
 504 |     train_idx = indices[:split]
 505 |     val_idx = indices[split:]
 506 |     return X[train_idx], X[val_idx], y[train_idx], y[val_idx]
 507 | 
 508 | 
 509 | def load_all_datasets(
 510 |     root: Path = DATA_ROOT,
 511 | ) -> list[tuple[str, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]:
 512 |     """Load all datasets and return list of (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val)."""
 513 |     entries = discover_datasets(root)
 514 |     results = []
 515 |     for name, path in entries:
 516 |         try:
 517 |             X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = load_dataset(path)
 518 |             results.append((name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val))
 519 |         except Exception as exc:
 520 |             print(f"Warning: skipping dataset {name}: {exc}")
 521 |     return results
 522 | 
 523 | 
 524 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 525 | PARSIMONY_WEIGHT = float(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_WEIGHT", "0.20"))
 526 | PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE = float(
 527 |     os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE", "160")
 528 | )
 529 | 
 530 | 
 531 | def _parsimony_penalty_factor(complexity: float) -> float:
 532 |     """Bounded penalty factor for expression complexity."""
 533 |     if not np.isfinite(complexity) or PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE <= 0:
 534 |         return 1.0
 535 |     normalized = min(max(float(complexity), 0.0) / PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE, 1.0)
 536 |     return max(0.0, 1.0 - PARSIMONY_WEIGHT * normalized)
 537 | 
 538 | 
 539 | def _aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 540 |     """Aggregate per-dataset metrics without letting failed datasets disappear."""
 541 |     nmse_vals = [r["nmse_val"] for r in per_dataset_results.values()]
 542 |     complexity_vals = []
 543 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 544 |         try:
 545 |             value = float(r.get("complexity", float("nan")))
 546 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 547 |             continue
 548 |         if np.isfinite(value):
 549 |             complexity_vals.append(value)
 550 |     aggregate_complexity = float(max(complexity_vals)) if complexity_vals else 0.0
 551 | 
 552 |     violation_errors = sorted(
 553 |         {
 554 |             str(r.get("error"))
 555 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 556 |             if r.get("error") and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in str(r.get("error"))
 557 |         }
 558 |     )
 559 |     failed_datasets = sorted(
 560 |         name
 561 |         for name, result in per_dataset_results.items()
 562 |         if result.get("error") or not np.isfinite(float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf"))))
 563 |     )
 564 |     templates = sorted(
 565 |         {
 566 |             str(r.get("equation_template"))
 567 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 568 |             if r.get("combined_score", 0.0) > 0 and r.get("equation_template")
 569 |         }
 570 |     )
 571 | 
 572 |     eq_template = ""
 573 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 574 |         if r.get("equation"):
 575 |             eq_template = r["equation"]
 576 |             break
 577 | 
 578 |     violation_error = None
 579 |     if violation_errors:
 580 |         violation_error = violation_errors[0]
 581 |     elif failed_datasets:
 582 |         examples = ", ".join(failed_datasets[:3])
 583 |         violation_error = (
 584 |             "Single-equation violation: candidate failed on "
 585 |             f"{len(failed_datasets)}/{len(per_dataset_results)} datasets. "
 586 |             "Failed datasets are not dropped from the aggregate reward. "
 587 |             f"Examples: {examples}"
 588 |         )
 589 |     elif len(templates) > 1:
 590 |         examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
 591 |         violation_error = (
 592 |             "Single-equation violation: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() produced "
 593 |             f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
 594 |             "The base equation must be identical for all datasets; only fitted "
 595 |             f"constants may vary. Example templates: {examples}"
 596 |         )
 597 | 
 598 |     if violation_error:
 599 |         agg_nmse = float("inf")
 600 |         agg_combined = 0.0
 601 |         n_successful = 0
 602 |     else:
 603 |         agg_nmse = float(np.mean(nmse_vals)) if nmse_vals else float("inf")
 604 |         raw_combined = (
 605 |             float(1.0 / (1.0 + max(agg_nmse, 0.0))) if np.isfinite(agg_nmse) else 0.0
 606 |         )
 607 |         parsimony_factor = _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity)
 608 |         agg_combined = raw_combined * parsimony_factor
 609 |         n_successful = sum(1 for value in nmse_vals if np.isfinite(value))
 610 | 
 611 |     aggregated = {
 612 |         "equation": eq_template,
 613 |         "nmse_val": agg_nmse,
 614 |         "combined_score": agg_combined,
 615 |         "fit_score": raw_combined if not violation_error else 0.0,
 616 |         "complexity": aggregate_complexity,
 617 |         "parsimony_weight": PARSIMONY_WEIGHT,
 618 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": (
 619 |             _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity) if not violation_error else 1.0
 620 |         ),
 621 |         "n_datasets": len(per_dataset_results),
 622 |         "n_successful": n_successful,
 623 |         "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 624 |         "per_dataset": per_dataset_results,
 625 |     }
 626 |     if violation_error:
 627 |         aggregated["error"] = violation_error
 628 |     return aggregated
 629 | 
 630 | 
 631 | def evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path: str, timeout_seconds: int = 3000) -> dict:
 632 |     """Evaluate a candidate program across ALL datasets in a subprocess.
 633 | 
 634 |     The subprocess loads every dataset, calls the candidate's
 635 |     evaluate_symbolic_candidate() once per dataset (fitting independent
 636 |     constants each time) using parallel threads, and returns aggregated
 637 |     metrics.
 638 |     """
 639 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 640 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
 641 |     evaluator_path = str(Path(__file__).resolve())
 642 |     with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".py", delete=False) as temp_file:
 643 |         script = f"""
 644 | import importlib.util
 645 | import os
 646 | import pickle
 647 | import sys
 648 | import traceback
 649 | from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
 650 | 
 651 | os.environ.setdefault("OMP_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 652 | os.environ.setdefault("MKL_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 653 | os.environ.setdefault("OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 654 | 
 655 | import numpy as np
 656 | 
 657 | sys.path.insert(0, {harness_root!r})
 658 | 
 659 | program_path = {program_path!r}
 660 | evaluator_path = {evaluator_path!r}
 661 | results_path = {temp_file.name + ".results"!r}
 662 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 663 | 
 664 | def _write_payload_and_exit(payload):
 665 |     with open(results_path, "wb") as f:
 666 |         pickle.dump(payload, f)
 667 |     sys.stdout.flush()
 668 |     sys.stderr.flush()
 669 |     # PySR/PythonCall can leave Julia runtime threads/finalizers alive at
 670 |     # interpreter shutdown. The parent only needs the pickle payload.
 671 |     os._exit(0)
 672 | 
 673 | try:
 674 |     evaluator_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("benchmark_evaluator", evaluator_path)
 675 |     evaluator = importlib.util.module_from_spec(evaluator_spec)
 676 |     evaluator_spec.loader.exec_module(evaluator)
 677 |     evaluator.validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 678 | 
 679 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
 680 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
 681 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
 682 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
 683 |         single_equation_evaluation,
 684 |         validate_single_equation_result,
 685 |     )
 686 | 
 687 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
 688 |         program, "run_discovery", None
 689 |     )
 690 |     if fn is None:
 691 |         raise AttributeError("Program must define evaluate_symbolic_candidate() or run_discovery()")
 692 | 
 693 |     datasets = evaluator.load_all_datasets()
 694 | 
 695 |     def _eval_one(args):
 696 |         ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = args
 697 |         try:
 698 |             with single_equation_evaluation():
 699 |                 result = fn(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val)
 700 |                 validate_single_equation_result(result)
 701 |             ds_nmse = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
 702 |             ds_combined = float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0))
 703 |             return ds_name, {{
 704 |                 "nmse_val": ds_nmse,
 705 |                 "combined_score": ds_combined,
 706 |                 "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
 707 |                 "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
 708 |                 "equation_template": str(result.get("equation_template", "")),
 709 |                 "constants": result.get("constants", {{}}),
 710 |             }}
 711 |         except Exception as exc:
 712 |             return ds_name, {{
 713 |                 "nmse_val": float("inf"),
 714 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
 715 |                 "error": str(exc),
 716 |             }}
 717 | 
 718 |     per_dataset_results = {{}}
 719 |     with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
 720 |         futures = [executor.submit(_eval_one, dataset) for dataset in datasets]
 721 |         for future in as_completed(futures):
 722 |             ds_name, ds_result = future.result()
 723 |             per_dataset_results[ds_name] = ds_result
 724 | 
 725 |     aggregated = evaluator._aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results)
 726 | 
 727 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"result": aggregated}})
 728 | except Exception as exc:
 729 |     traceback.print_exc()
 730 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"error": str(exc)}})
 731 | """
 732 |         temp_file.write(script.encode())
 733 |         temp_path = temp_file.name
 734 | 
 735 |     results_path = f"{temp_path}.results"
 736 |     try:
 737 |         process = subprocess.Popen(
 738 |             [sys.executable, temp_path],
 739 |             stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
 740 |             stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
 741 |         )
 742 |         stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=timeout_seconds)
 743 |         if stdout:
 744 |             print(stdout.decode())
 745 |         if stderr:
 746 |             print(stderr.decode())
 747 |         if process.returncode != 0:
 748 |             raise RuntimeError(f"Subprocess exited with code {process.returncode}")
 749 |         if not os.path.exists(results_path):
 750 |             raise RuntimeError("Results file not found")
 751 |         with open(results_path, "rb") as f:
 752 |             payload = pickle.load(f)
 753 |         if "error" in payload:
 754 |             raise RuntimeError(payload["error"])
 755 |         return payload["result"]
 756 |     except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
 757 |         process.kill()
 758 |         process.wait()
 759 |         raise TimeoutError(f"Program execution timed out after {timeout_seconds}s")
 760 |     finally:
 761 |         for path in (temp_path, results_path):
 762 |             if os.path.exists(path):
 763 |                 os.unlink(path)
 764 | 
 765 | 
 766 | def _metrics_from_result(result: dict, eval_time: float) -> dict:
 767 |     """Normalize aggregated harness output into SkyDiscover evaluator metrics."""
 768 |     nmse_val = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
 769 |     combined = float(result.get("combined_score", combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val)))
 770 |     if not np.isfinite(combined):
 771 |         combined = 0.0
 772 | 
 773 |     return {
 774 |         "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
 775 |         "loss": nmse_val,
 776 |         "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
 777 |         "nmse_train": float("inf"),
 778 |         "nmse_val": nmse_val,
 779 |         "combined_score": combined,
 780 |         "fit_score": float(result.get("fit_score", combined)),
 781 |         "parsimony_weight": float(result.get("parsimony_weight", PARSIMONY_WEIGHT)),
 782 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": float(result.get("parsimony_penalty_factor", 1.0)),
 783 |         "eval_time": float(eval_time),
 784 |         "n_datasets": result.get("n_datasets", 0),
 785 |         "n_successful": result.get("n_successful", 0),
 786 |         "eval_workers": result.get("eval_workers", NUM_WORKERS),
 787 |         "per_dataset": result.get("per_dataset", {}),
 788 |         "error": str(result.get("error", "")),
 789 |     }
 790 | 
 791 | 
 792 | def evaluate(program_path: str) -> dict:
 793 |     """Evaluate one evolved symbolic-equation candidate across all datasets."""
 794 |     try:
 795 |         start = time.time()
 796 |         result = evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path, timeout_seconds=3000)
 797 |         metrics = _metrics_from_result(result, time.time() - start)
 798 |         print(
 799 |             "Evaluation: "
 800 |             f"nmse_val={metrics['nmse_val']:.6f}, "
 801 |             f"combined_score={metrics['combined_score']:.6f}, "
 802 |             f"n_datasets={metrics['n_datasets']}, "
 803 |             f"n_successful={metrics['n_successful']}, "
 804 |             f"time={metrics['eval_time']:.2f}s"
 805 |         )
 806 |         print(f"Equation template: {metrics['equation']}")
 807 |         if metrics.get("error"):
 808 |             print(f"Evaluator feedback: {metrics['error']}")
 809 |         return metrics
 810 |     except Exception as exc:
 811 |         print(f"Evaluation failed: {exc}")
 812 |         traceback.print_exc()
 813 |         return {
 814 |             "equation": "",
 815 |             "loss": float("inf"),
 816 |             "complexity": 0.0,
 817 |             "nmse_train": float("inf"),
 818 |             "nmse_val": float("inf"),
 819 |             "combined_score": 0.0,
 820 |             "eval_time": 0.0,
 821 |             "n_datasets": 0,
 822 |             "n_successful": 0,
 823 |             "per_dataset": {},
 824 |             "error": str(exc),
 825 |         }
 826 | 
 827 | 
 828 | def _eval_stage1_worker(args):
 829 |     """Module-level worker for stage 1 parallel evaluation."""
 830 |     name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path = args
 831 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
 832 |     if harness_root not in sys.path:
 833 |         sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
 834 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 835 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
 836 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
 837 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
 838 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
 839 |         single_equation_evaluation,
 840 |         validate_single_equation_result,
 841 |     )
 842 | 
 843 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
 844 |         program, "run_discovery", None
 845 |     )
 846 |     if fn is None:
 847 |         return 0.0, "missing evaluate_symbolic_candidate()", ""
 848 |     n = min(200, len(X_train))
 849 |     m = min(80, len(X_val))
 850 |     try:
 851 |         with single_equation_evaluation():
 852 |             result = fn(X_train[:n], y_train[:n], X_val[:m], y_val[:m])
 853 |             validate_single_equation_result(result)
 854 |         return float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0)), None, str(
 855 |             result.get("equation_template", "")
 856 |         )
 857 |     except Exception as exc:
 858 |         return 0.0, str(exc), ""
 859 | 
 860 | 
 861 | def evaluate_stage1(program_path: str) -> dict:
 862 |     """Fast cascade stage: evaluate on a small subset of datasets with reduced data."""
 863 |     try:
 864 |         harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
 865 |         if harness_root not in sys.path:
 866 |             sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
 867 | 
 868 |         all_datasets = load_all_datasets()
 869 |         rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
 870 |         if len(all_datasets) > 8:
 871 |             indices = rng.choice(len(all_datasets), size=8, replace=False)
 872 |             subset = [all_datasets[i] for i in sorted(indices)]
 873 |         else:
 874 |             subset = all_datasets
 875 | 
 876 |         work_items = [
 877 |             (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path)
 878 |             for name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in subset
 879 |         ]
 880 | 
 881 |         with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
 882 |             futures = [executor.submit(_eval_stage1_worker, item) for item in work_items]
 883 |             worker_results = [future.result() for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
 884 | 
 885 |         combined_scores = [score for score, _error, _template in worker_results]
 886 |         successful_scores = [score for score in combined_scores if score > 0]
 887 |         errors = sorted({error for _score, error, _template in worker_results if error})
 888 |         violation_errors = sorted(
 889 |             {
 890 |                 error
 891 |                 for _score, error, _template in worker_results
 892 |                 if error and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in error
 893 |             }
 894 |         )
 895 |         if violation_errors:
 896 |             return {
 897 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
 898 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
 899 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
 900 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 901 |                 "error": violation_errors[0],
 902 |             }
 903 |         if errors:
 904 |             return {
 905 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
 906 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
 907 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
 908 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 909 |                 "error": (
 910 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 candidate failed on "
 911 |                     f"{len(errors)} dataset evaluations. Example: {errors[0]}"
 912 |                 ),
 913 |             }
 914 |         templates = sorted(
 915 |             {
 916 |                 template
 917 |                 for score, _error, template in worker_results
 918 |                 if score > 0 and template
 919 |             }
 920 |         )
 921 |         if len(templates) > 1:
 922 |             examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
 923 |             return {
 924 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
 925 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
 926 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
 927 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 928 |                 "error": (
 929 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 produced "
 930 |                     f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
 931 |                     "The base equation must be identical for all datasets. "
 932 |                     f"Example templates: {examples}"
 933 |                 ),
 934 |             }
 935 |         agg_combined = float(np.mean(combined_scores)) if combined_scores else 0.0
 936 |         return {
 937 |             "combined_score": agg_combined,
 938 |             "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
 939 |             "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
 940 |             "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 941 |         }
 942 |     except Exception as exc:
 943 |         print(f"Stage 1 evaluation failed: {exc}")
 944 |         return {"combined_score": 0.0, "error": str(exc)}
 945 | 
 946 | 
 947 | def evaluate_stage2(program_path: str) -> dict:
 948 |     """Full evaluation for cascade stage 2."""
 949 |     return evaluate(program_path)
 950 | 
 951 | 
 952 | if __name__ == "__main__":
 953 |     if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "--list-datasets":
 954 |         datasets = discover_datasets()
 955 |         print(f"Found {len(datasets)} datasets:")
 956 |         for name, path in datasets:
 957 |             try:
 958 |                 X, y = _load_fit_table(path)
 959 |                 print(f"  {name}: {len(y)} samples, {X.shape[1]} features")
 960 |             except Exception as exc:
 961 |                 print(f"  {name}: FAILED - {exc}")
 962 |     else:
 963 |         default_program = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "initial_program.py"
 964 |         path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else str(default_program)
 965 |         print(evaluate(path))

  99 |         "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '.')\nfrom disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets\nimport numpy as np\nimport sympy as sp\nfrom scipy.optimize import least_squares\n\ndatasets = load_all_datasets()\n\n# Test the best known expression (Program 3/4) manually on a few hard datasets\n# Template: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c7*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)\n# initial: [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0, 0.0]\n\ndef predict(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c\n    log_time = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c2**2 + 1e-30))\n    log_param = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7\n    half_log_time = c3 * log_param + c5\n    sig_arg = rate * (log_time - half_log_time)\n    return c0 * np.tanh(sig_arg) + c4 + c6 * np.tanh(x0)\n\n# Test on Kar2011 (hardest - very large x0)\nhard_names = ['htt/Kar2011', 'serum amyloid', 'biofilm proteins/TasA']\nfor ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n    for h in hard_names:\n        if h in ds_name:\n            x0_init = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0, 0.0]\n            def residual(c):\n                try:\n                    p = predict(X_train, c)\n                    if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n                    return p - y_train\n                except: return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            res = least_squares(residual, x0_init, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n            p_val = predict(X_val, res.x)\n            nmse = np.mean((p_val - y_val)**2) / (np.var(y_val) + 1e-10)\n            print(f'{ds_name[:55]:55s} nmse_val={nmse:.4f} c={[f\\\"{v:.3f}\\\" for v in res.x]}')\n            break\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

y_train\n        \n        res = least_squares(residual_lin, [0.5, 1e-5, 400000, 0.5], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n        pv = pred_linear(X_val, res.x)\n        nmse = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        print(f'Linear tanh: nmse_val={nmse:.4f}')\n        \n        # Test tanh in sqrt(x0) space\n        def pred_sqrt(X, c):\n            x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n            return c[0]*np.tanh(c[1]*(np.sqrt(np.maximum(x0,0)) - c[2])) + c[3]\n        \n        def residual_sqrt(c):\n            p = pred_sqrt(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        \n        res2 = least_squares(residual_sqrt, [0.5, 0.001, 500, 0.5], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n        pv2 = pred_sqrt(X_val, res2.x)\n        nmse2 = np.mean((pv2-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        print(f'Sqrt tanh: nmse_val={nmse2:.4f}')\n        \n        # Test tanh in x0^c5 space (power law)\n        def pred_pow(X, c):\n            x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n            return c[0]*np.tanh(c[1]*(np.maximum(x0,1)**c[4] - c[2])) + c[3]\n        \n        def residual_pow(c):\n            p = pred_pow(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        \n        res3 = least_squares(residual_pow, [0.5, 1.0, 100.0, 0.5, 0.5], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)\n        pv3 = pred_pow(X_val, res3.x)\n        nmse3 = np.mean((pv3-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        print(f'Power tanh: nmse_val={nmse3:.4f} exponent={res3.x[4]:.3f}')\n        break\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

half)) + c[6]\n        \n        def res_quad(c):\n            p = pred_quad(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        \n        r3 = least_squares(res_quad, [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.01, 12.0, 0.5, 0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)\n        pv3 = pred_quad(X_val, r3.x)\n        nmse3 = np.mean((pv3-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        print(f'Quad x1 scaling: nmse_val={nmse3:.4f}')\n        \n        # Try: asinh time with x1^2 half-time (quadratic concentration effect)\n        def pred_asinh_quad(X, c):\n            x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n            lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c[2]**2 + 1e-30))\n            half = c[3]*np.log(np.maximum(x1,0.1)) + c[4]*x1 + c[5]\n            rate = c[1]\n            return c[0]*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c[6]\n        \n        def res_aq(c):\n            p = pred_asinh_quad(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        \n        r4 = least_squares(res_aq, [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.01, 12.0, 0.5], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)\n        pv4 = pred_asinh_quad(X_val, r4.x)\n        nmse4 = np.mean((pv4-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        print(f'Asinh + quad x1: nmse_val={nmse4:.4f}')\n        break\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

(coeffs_k[0]*lx1_k + coeffs_k[1])}')\n\n# Kar2011 has non-monotonic half-times - the log^2 term helps here\n# Check: what does log^2 fit give?\nA_k2 = np.column_stack([lx1_k, lx1_k**2, np.ones_like(lx1_k)])\ncoeffs_k2, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(A_k2, lht_k, rcond=None)\nprint(f'Kar2011 with log^2: {coeffs_k2[0]:.3f}*log(x1) + {coeffs_k2[1]:.4f}*log(x1)^2 + {coeffs_k2[2]:.3f}')\nresid_k2 = lht_k - y_train\n                try:\n                    rD = least_squares(res_D, [0.5,1.0,1.0,0.5,c4_init,c5_init,0.5,0.0,0.0], \n                                      loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)\n                    pvD = pred_D(X_val, rD.x)\n                    nmD = np.mean((pvD-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n                    if nmD < best_nm:\n                        best_nm = nmD\n                        best_c = rD.x\n                except: pass\n        print(f'D best: nmse={best_nm:.4f} c3={best_c[3]:.4f} c4={best_c[4]:.4f} c5={best_c[5]:.4f}')\n        \n        # The issue: for insulin x1=[344,861,1722,3444], log(x1)=[5.84,6.76,7.45,8.14]\n        # log^2(x1) = [34.1, 45.7, 55.5, 66.3] -- large values\n        # If c4 is even slightly nonzero, the half-time changes dramatically across x1 values\n        # The optimizer with single init [c4=0] may not converge properly\n        # Solution: c4 init should be 0.0 and c5 should compensate\n        \n        # What's the analytical best for D?\n        # For insulin: half_time(x1) = exp(c3*log(x1) + c4*log(x1)^2 + c5)\n        # At x1=344: log(x1)=5.84, half_time~15737 -> c3*5.84 + c4*34.1 + c5 = log(15737)=9.66\n        # At x1=3444: log(x1)=8.14, half_time~4823 -> c3*8.14 + c4*66.3 + c5 = log(4823)=8.48\n        # Difference: c3*2.3 + c4*32.2 = -1.18 -> c3 + c4*14 = -0.513\n        # With c3=-0.515 (from linear fit), c4=0 satisfies this well\n        # So c4 should converge to ~0 for insulin\n        print(f'Expected c4~0 for insulin, got c4={best_c[4]:.4f}')\n        break\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

 219 |         "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '.')\nfrom disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import least_squares\nimport sympy as sp\n\ndatasets = load_all_datasets()\n\n# Model E: Drop x1^c8 rate (set rate=c1 constant) AND keep log^2(x1) half-time\n# c0*tanh(c1*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c4*log(x1)^2 - c5)) + c6 + c7*tanh(x0)\n# 8 constants, simpler rate\n\n# Model F: Keep x1^c8 rate, drop tanh(x0) secondary ramp, keep log^2(x1)\n# c0*tanh(c1*x1^c8*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c4*log(x1)^2 - c5)) + c6\n# 7 constants, no secondary ramp\n\n# Model G: Drop x1^c8 rate AND drop tanh(x0) ramp, keep log^2(x1)\n# c0*tanh(c1*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c4*log(x1)^2 - c5)) + c6\n# 7 constants, simplest\n\ndef pred_E(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c\n    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c2**2 + 1e-30))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    half = c3*lx1 + c4*lx1**2 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(c1*(lx0 - half)) + c6 + c7*np.tanh(x0)\n\ndef pred_F(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c\n    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c2**2 + 1e-30))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7\n    half = c3*lx1 + c4*lx1**2 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c6\n\ndef pred_G(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c\n    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c2**2 + 1e-30))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    half = c3*lx1 + c4*lx1**2 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(c1*(lx0 - half)) + c6\n\n# Compute complexities\nx0s, x1s = sp.Symbol('x0'), sp.Symbol('x1')\ncs = [sp.Symbol(f'c{i}') for i in range(10)]\ndef complexity(expr):\n    return sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(expr))\n\nlx0 = sp.log(x0s + sp.sqrt(x0s**2 + cs[2]**2))\nlx1 = sp.log(x1s)\n\nexprE = cs[0]*sp.tanh(cs[1]*(lx0 - cs[3]*lx1 - cs[4]*lx1**2 - cs[5])) + cs[6] + cs[7]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nexprF = cs[0]*sp.tanh(cs[1]*x1s**cs[7]*(lx0 - cs[3]*lx1 - cs[4]*lx1**2 - cs[5])) + cs[6]\nexprG = cs[0]*sp.tanh(cs[1]*(lx0 - cs[3]*lx1 - cs[4]*lx1**2 - cs[5])) + cs[6]\n\nprint(f'E complexity: {complexity(exprE)}, F complexity: {complexity(exprF)}, G complexity: {complexity(exprG)}')\n\ninit_E = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 0.5, 0.0]\ninit_F = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 0.5, 0.0]\ninit_G = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 0.5]\n\nfor label, pred_fn, init in [('E', pred_E, init_E), ('F', pred_F, init_F), ('G', pred_G, init_G)]:\n    nmse_list = []\n    for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n        def res(c, pf=pred_fn):\n            p = pf(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        r = least_squares(res, init, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n        pv = pred_fn(X_val, r.x)\n        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        nmse_list.append(nm)\n    mean_nm = np.mean(nmse_list)\n    if label == 'E': cx = complexity(exprE)\n    elif label == 'F': cx = complexity(exprF)\n    else: cx = complexity(exprG)\n    parsimony = max(0, 1 - 0.2*(cx/160))\n    combined = (1/(1+mean_nm)) * parsimony\n    print(f'Model {label}: mean_nmse={mean_nm:.4f} complexity={cx} parsimony={parsimony:.4f} combined={combined:.4f}')\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

 243 |         "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '.')\nfrom disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import least_squares\nimport sympy as sp\n\ndatasets = load_all_datasets()\n\n# The best known is A with complexity=35, combined=0.9306\n# Can we find something with complexity=33 or 31 but mean_nmse < 0.028?\n# \n# Key: what if we use log(x0+c2) instead of log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2))?\n# log(x0+c2): complexity is much lower but only defined for x0 > -c2\n# For negative x0 datasets (TasA x0_min=-1565), c2 must be > 1565 -- optimizer can find this\n# This saves 4 nodes vs asinh form\n\n# Model H: log(x0+c2) instead of asinh\n# c0*tanh(c1*x1^c7*(log(x0+c2) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)\ndef pred_H(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c\n    lx0 = np.log(np.maximum(x0 + c2, 1e-10))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7\n    half = c3*lx1 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c6*np.tanh(x0)\n\n# Model I: exp-based logistic (1/(1+exp(-z))) instead of tanh\n# Actually tanh(z) = 2*sigmoid(2z)-1, same complexity\n# \n# Model J: use log1p(x0/c2) instead - log(1+x0/c2) = log((c2+x0)/c2)\n# This is just log(x0+c2) - log(c2), so equivalent to H with c5 absorbing log(c2)\n\n# Let's check complexity of H\nx0s, x1s = sp.Symbol('x0'), sp.Symbol('x1')\nc = [sp.Symbol(f'c{i}') for i in range(8)]\nexprH = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s+c[2]) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\ncxH = sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(exprH))\nprint(f'Model H complexity: {cxH}')\nparsimonyH = max(0, 1 - 0.2*(cxH/160))\n\n# Test H on all datasets\ninit_H = [0.5, 1.0, 1600.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0, 0.0]  # c2=1600 to handle negative x0\nnmse_H = []\nresults_H = []\nfor ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n    def res_H(c):\n        p = pred_H(X_train, c)\n        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n        return p - y_train\n    rH = least_squares(res_H, init_H, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n    pvH = pred_H(X_val, rH.x)\n    nmH = np.mean((pvH-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n    nmse_H.append(nmH)\n    results_H.append((ds_name, nmH))\n\nmean_H = np.mean(nmse_H)\ncombH = (1/(1+mean_H)) * parsimonyH\nprint(f'Model H (log(x0+c2)): mean_nmse={mean_H:.4f} complexity={cxH} parsimony={parsimonyH:.4f} combined={combH:.4f}')\nresults_H.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)\nfor n,v in results_H[:5]:\n    print(f'  {n[:50]:50s} {v:.4f}')\nprint()\n\n# Also try H with c2 init = 1.0 (let optimizer find it)\nnmse_H2 = []\nfor ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n    def res_H2(c):\n        p = pred_H(X_train, c)\n        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n        return p - y_train\n    rH2 = least_squares(res_H2, [0.5,1.0,1.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n    pvH2 = pred_H(X_val, rH2.x)\n    nmH2 = np.mean((pvH2-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n    nmse_H2.append(nmH2)\nmean_H2 = np.mean(nmse_H2)\ncombH2 = (1/(1+mean_H2)) * parsimonyH\nprint(f'Model H (c2_init=1): mean_nmse={mean_H2:.4f} combined={combH2:.4f}')\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

 251 |         "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '.')\nfrom disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import least_squares\nimport sympy as sp\n\ndatasets = load_all_datasets()\n\n# The key difference between parent (0.8631) and Program 3 (0.9306):\n# Parent: c5=5.0 init, no tanh(x0) secondary ramp\n# Program 3: c5=10.0 init, HAS tanh(x0) secondary ramp\n# \n# Let's verify: does adding tanh(x0) back to the parent structure improve it?\n# Parent structure: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c6*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4\n# With c5=5.0 init, 7 constants, complexity=31\n\ndef pred_parent(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c\n    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + c2**2 + 1e-30))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6\n    half = c3*lx1 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4\n\n# Test parent with different c5 inits\nfor c5_init in [5.0, 10.0]:\n    init = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.0, c5_init, 0.0]\n    nmse_list = []\n    for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n        def res(c):\n            p = pred_parent(X_train, c)\n            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n            return p - y_train\n        r = least_squares(res, init, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n        pv = pred_parent(X_val, r.x)\n        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n        nmse_list.append(nm)\n    mean_nm = np.mean(nmse_list)\n    # complexity=31\n    parsimony = max(0, 1 - 0.2*(31/160))\n    combined = (1/(1+mean_nm)) * parsimony\n    print(f'Parent c5_init={c5_init}: mean_nmse={mean_nm:.4f} combined={combined:.4f}')\n\nprint()\n# Now test: what's the exact complexity of Program 3/4?\nx0s, x1s = sp.Symbol('x0'), sp.Symbol('x1')\nc = [sp.Symbol(f'c{i}') for i in range(8)]\nlx0 = sp.log(x0s + sp.sqrt(x0s**2 + c[2]**2))\nlx1 = sp.log(x1s)\nrate = c[1] * x1s**c[7]\nhalf = c[3]*lx1 + c[5]\nsig_arg = rate * (lx0 - saves 1 node\n# log(x0 + sqrt(x0^2 + c2))  -- but c2 must be positive for real sqrt\nexprA2 = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s + sp.sqrt(x0s**2 + c[2])) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\ncxA2 = sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(exprA2))\nprint(f'Model A with c2 (not c2^2) complexity: {cxA2}')\n\n# What about dropping tanh(x0) but keeping c5=10 init?\nexprA3 = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[6]*(sp.log(x0s + sp.sqrt(x0s**2 + c[2]**2)) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4]\ncxA3 = sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(exprA3))\nprint(f'Model A without tanh(x0) complexity: {cxA3}')\n\n# Test parent with c5=10 init (key improvement from parent to Program 3/4)\ninit_p10 = [0.5, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.0, 10.0, 0.0]\nnmse_p10 = []\nfor ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:\n    def res(c):\n        p = pred_parent(X_train, c)\n        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)\n        return p - y_train\n    r = least_squares(res, init_p10, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)\n    pv = pred_parent(X_val, r.x)\n    nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)\n    nmse_p10.append(nm)\nmean_p10 = np.mean(nmse_p10)\ncomb_p10 = (1/(1+mean_p10)) * max(0, 1 - saves 2 complexity nodes (35->33)\n# c0*tanh(c1*x1^c7*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2)) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)\n# c2 must be >= 0 for real sqrt - optimizer should handle this\n# But c2 can be negative during optimization -> sqrt(x0^2+c2) might be complex\n# Risk: if c2 < -x0_min^2, sqrt is imaginary\n# For TasA x0_min=-1565, need c2 > -1565^2 = -2.45M -- unlikely to be violated\n\ndef pred_A2(X, c):\n    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]\n    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c\n    # Use abs(c2) to ensure non-negative under sqrt\n    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(np.maximum(x0**2 + c2, 1e-30)))\n    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))\n    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7\n    half = c3*lx1 + c5\n    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - not worth it\n# Better: use c2**2 but write it as c2*c2 - same complexity\n\n# What about a completely different approach to reduce complexity?\n# Use log(x0*c2 + 1) = log1p(c2*x0) -- this is log(1+c2*x0)\n# For large x0: log(c2*x0) = log(c2) + log(x0), absorbed by c5\n# For negative x0: need c2*x0 > -1, so c2 > -1/x0_min\n# With x0_min=-1565: c2 > 1/1565 = 0.00064 -- very small, optimizer will handle\nexprA3 = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(c[2]*x0s + 1) -  275 |         "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '.')\nimport sympy as sp\n\nx0s, x1s = sp.Symbol('x0'), sp.Symbol('x1')\nc = [sp.Symbol(f'c{i}') for i in range(8)]\n\ndef cx(expr):\n    return sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(expr))\n\n# Current best (A): complexity=35\nexprA = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s+sp.sqrt(x0s**2+c[2]**2)) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'A (c2^2): {cx(exprA)}')\n\n# Idea: factor out the rate differently to reduce nodes\n# c0*tanh((c1*x1^c7*log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*x1^c7*log(x1)*c1 - c1*c5*x1^c7))\n# This is the same expression, just written differently - same complexity\n\n# What if we merge c1 and c0 into one term? c0 and c1 are both free constants\n# The expression c0*tanh(c1*...) - c0 scales the output, c1 scales the input\n# We could write: c0*tanh(c1*(log_time - c3*log_param - c5)) \n# where c1 already incorporates the x1^c7 term implicitly\n# But that loses x1-dependent rate...\n\n# Key idea: what if we write rate = c1 + c7*log(x1) instead of c1*x1^c7?\n# This is a log-linear rate instead of power-law\n# For single-x1 datasets: log(x1) is constant, absorbed by c5\n# For multi-x1: provides concentration-dependent rate\nexprA_lograte = c[0]*sp.tanh((c[1]+c[7]*sp.log(x1s))*(sp.log(x0s+sp.sqrt(x0s**2+c[2]**2)) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'A lograte: {cx(exprA_lograte)}')\n\n# What if rate = c1*exp(c7*log(x1)) = c1*x1^c7 but written differently? Same complexity.\n\n# Idea: c0*tanh(c1*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2))/c3 - log(x1) - c5))\n# = c0*tanh(c1/c3*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c3*c5))\n# Saves 0 nodes\n\n# What if we use x0^2 directly without c2^2?\n# log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+1)) - just 1 instead of c2^2 - saves 2 nodes\nexprA_c2fixed = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s+sp.sqrt(x0s**2+1)) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'A c2=1 fixed: {cx(exprA_c2fixed)}')\n\n# What about using sinh instead of tanh? sinh(x) = (e^x - e^-x)/2\n# No - tanh is better bounded\n\n# What about: c0/(1+exp(-c1*x1^c7*(log(x0+sqrt(x0^2+c2^2)) - c3*log(x1) - c5))) + c4\n# = logistic form - same complexity as tanh version\n\n# What about: c0*tanh(c1*(x0^c2 - c3*x1^c4 - c5)) + c6 + c7*tanh(x0)\n# Power law time instead of log time\nexprPow = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*(x0s**c[2] - c[3]*x1s**c[4] - c[5])) + c[6] + c[7]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'Power law: {cx(exprPow)}')\n\n# What about: c0*tanh(c1*(log(x0+c2) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)\n# log(x0+c2) instead of asinh -- saves sqrt nodes\nexprLogShift = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s+c[2]) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'log(x0+c2): {cx(exprLogShift)}')\n\n# What about: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c7*(log(x0+c2) - c3*log(x1+c4) - c5)) + c6\n# Using log(x1+c4) instead of log(x1) - handles x1=0 case\nexprLS2 = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*x1s**c[7]*(sp.log(x0s+c[2]) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s+c[4]) - c[5])) + c[6]\nprint(f'log(x0+c2), log(x1+c4), no tanh(x0): {cx(exprLS2)}')\n\n# What about: c0*tanh(c1*(log(x0+c2) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)  [no x1^c7]\nexprLS3 = c[0]*sp.tanh(c[1]*(sp.log(x0s+c[2]) - c[3]*sp.log(x1s) - c[5])) + c[4] + c[6]*sp.tanh(x0s)\nprint(f'log(x0+c2), no x1^c7: {cx(exprLS3)}')\n\" 2>/dev/null\n"

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares
import sympy as sp

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# Current best (Program 3/4): c0*tanh(c1*x1^c7*(asinh(x0/c2) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c6*tanh(x0)
# c2=1 fixed version (Program 3): complexity=33, combined=0.9333
# Let's test the exact Program 3 structure and identify worst datasets

def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

init_P3 = [0.5, 1.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0]
results = []
for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    def res(c):
        p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
        return p - y_train
    r = least_squares(res, init_P3, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
    pv = pred_P3(X_val, r.x)
    nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
    results.append((ds_name, nm, r.x))

results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
mean_nm = np.mean([r[1] for r in results])
print(f'P3 mean_nmse={mean_nm:.4f} fit={1/(1+mean_nm):.4f}')
print('Top 10 worst:')
for n,v,c in results[:10]:
    print(f'  {n[:55]:55s} {v:.4f}  c2={c[2]:.3f} c6={c[6]:.3f}')
" 2>/dev/null

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# Investigate Kar2011 datasets
for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kar2011' in ds_name and 'Q37_prim' in ds_name:
        x0, x1 = X_train[:,0], X_train[:,1]
        print(f'Dataset: {ds_name}')
        print(f'x0=[{x0.min():.0f},{x0.max():.0f}], x1 unique={np.unique(x1)}')
        print(f'n_train={len(y_train)}, n_val={len(y_val)}')
        # Show half-times per concentration
        for conc in np.unique(x1):
            mask = x1 == conc
            x0c = x0[mask]; yc = y_train[mask]
            idx = np.argsort(x0c)
            x0s, ys = x0c[idx], yc[idx]
            diffs = np.abs(ys - 0.5)
            hi = np.argmin(diffs)
            print(f'  x1={conc:.0f}: half_time~{x0s[hi]:.0f}s, y_max={ys.max():.3f}')
        break

print()
# Test: what if we use x0^c8 (power law time) instead of asinh?
def pred_pow(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7
    # half-time: c3*log(x1) + c4*x1^c5 + c6  -- power law x1 half-time
    half = c3*lx1 + c4*np.maximum(x1,1e-10)**c5 + c6
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half))

# Test on Kar2011_Q37_prim
for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kar2011_Q37_prim' in ds_name:
        for c5_init in [0.5, 1.0, 2.0]:
            def res(c):
                p = pred_pow(X_train, c)
                if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
                return p - y_train
            r = least_squares(res, [0.5,1.0,0.5,0.5,1.0,c5_init,10.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)
            pv = pred_pow(X_val, r.x)
            nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
            print(f'Power half-time c5_init={c5_init}: nmse={nm:.4f} c={r.x.round(3)}')
        break

09:32:03 [agentic_generator] Step 6: tool=run_command returned:

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# Key insight: Kar2011 half-times are NON-MONOTONIC in x1
# x1=[3,4,6,8,9,22], half_times=[111k,130k,374k,308k,252k,186k]
# No simple monotonic function of x1 can capture this
# The best we can do is fit the AVERAGE across concentrations
# 
# Let's check: what is the BEST achievable NMSE on Kar2011 with any smooth function?
# Try: tanh in log(x0) with NO x1 dependence (just average)
def pred_nox1(X, c):
    x0 = X[:,0]
    c0,c1,c2,c3 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    return c0*np.tanh(c1*(lx0 - c2)) + c3

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kar2011_Q37_prim' in ds_name:
        def res(c):
            p = pred_nox1(X_train, c)
            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
            return p - y_train
        r = least_squares(res, [0.5, 1.0, 12.0, 0.5], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)
        pv = pred_nox1(X_val, r.x)
        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
        print(f'Kar2011_Q37_prim no-x1: nmse={nm:.4f}')
        break

# Now test: does max_nfev=2000 help P3 on Kar2011?
def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kar2011_Q37_prim' in ds_name:
        for nfev in [1000, 2000, 5000]:
            def res(c):
                p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
                if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
                return p - y_train
            r = least_squares(res, [0.5, 1.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=nfev)
            pv = pred_P3(X_val, r.x)
            nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
            print(f'P3 nfev={nfev}: nmse={nm:.4f} nfev_used={r.nfev}')
        break
" 2>/dev/null

P3 nfev=1000: nmse=0.2277 nfev_used=13

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# The Kar2011 NMSE=0.228 is near-irreducible for a single smooth function
# Let's check: can we improve the OTHER hard datasets?
# Kakkar2016_14kDa_Ex45_polyQ_sec: 0.0611
# Let's look at what's happening there

def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

# Check Kakkar datasets
for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kakkar2016_14kDa_Ex45_polyQ_sec' in ds_name:
        x0, x1 = X_train[:,0], X_train[:,1]
        print(f'Dataset: {ds_name}')
        print(f'x0=[{x0.min():.1f},{x0.max():.1f}], x1 unique={np.unique(x1)}, n={len(y_train)}')
        # Half-times per x1
        for conc in np.unique(x1):
            mask = x1 == conc
            x0c = x0[mask]; yc = y_train[mask]
            idx = np.argsort(x0c)
            x0s, ys = x0c[idx], yc[idx]
            diffs = np.abs(ys - 0.5)
            hi = np.argmin(diffs)
            print(f'  x1={conc:.1f}: half_time~{x0s[hi]:.1f}s, y_max={ys.max():.3f}, n={mask.sum()}')
        
        def res(c):
            p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
            return p - y_train
        r = least_squares(res, [0.5, 1.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
        pv = pred_P3(X_val, r.x)
        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
        print(f'P3 nmse={nm:.4f} c={r.x.round(3)}')
        break

print()
# Check if adding x1^2 term in half-time helps Kakkar
def pred_quad(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c7
    half = c3*lx1 + c4*lx1**2 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c6

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kakkar2016_14kDa_Ex45_polyQ_sec' in ds_name:
        def res(c):
            p = pred_quad(X_train, c)
            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
            return p - y_train
        r = least_squares(res, [0.5,1.0,0.5,0.0,10.0,0.5,0.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=2000)
        pv = pred_quad(X_val, r.x)
        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
        print(f'Quad half-time nmse={nm:.4f}')
        break

09:33:02 [agentic_generator] Step 8: tool=run_command returned:
$ python3 -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares
import sympy as sp

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# Test several structural variants across ALL 60 datasets
# P3: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c6*(asinh(x0) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c2*tanh(x0)  [7c, cx=33]
# P4: same but c2^2 in sqrt  [8c, cx=35]
# NEW: try replacing tanh(x0) with tanh(c7*x0) -- adds rate to secondary ramp
# This helps seeded datasets where tanh(x0) saturates too quickly

def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

# P3_scaled_ramp: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c6*(asinh(x0) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c2*tanh(c7*x0)
# 8 constants, complexity=35 (same as P4)
def pred_P3sr(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(c7*x0)

# P3_exp_ramp: c0*tanh(c1*x1^c6*(asinh(x0) - c3*log(x1) - c5)) + c4 + c2*(1-exp(-c7*x0))
# 8 constants, different ramp shape
def pred_P3er(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*(1 - np.exp(-np.maximum(c7,0)*x0))

nmse_P3, nmse_sr, nmse_er = [], [], []
better_sr, worse_sr = [], []

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    # P3
    def r3(c):
        p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
        return p - y_train
    rr3 = least_squares(r3, [0.5,1.0,0.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
    pv3 = pred_P3(X_val, rr3.x)
    nm3 = np.mean((pv3-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
    nmse_P3.append(nm3)

    # Scaled ramp
    def rsr(c):
        p = pred_P3sr(X_train, c)
        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
        return p - y_train
    rrsr = least_squares(rsr, [0.5,1.0,0.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0,1.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
    pvsr = pred_P3sr(X_val, rrsr.x)
    nmsr = np.mean((pvsr-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
    nmse_sr.append(nmsr)

    if nmsr > nm3 + 0.005: worse_sr.append((ds_name[:40], nm3, nmsr))
    if nm3 > nmsr + 0.005: better_sr.append((ds_name[:40], nm3, nmsr))

    # Exp ramp
    def rer(c):
        p = pred_P3er(X_train, c)
        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
        return p - y_train
    rrer = least_squares(rer, [0.5,1.0,0.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0,0.001], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
    pver = pred_P3er(X_val, rrer.x)
    nmer = np.mean((pver-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
    nmse_er.append(nmer)

print(f'P3:          mean={np.mean(nmse_P3):.4f}')
print(f'ScaledRamp:  mean={np.mean(nmse_sr):.4f}  (cx=35, parsimony=0.9563)')
print(f'ExpRamp:     mean={np.mean(nmse_er):.4f}  (cx=35)')
print(f'ScaledRamp better on {len(better_sr)} datasets:')
for n,a,b in sorted(better_sr, key=lambda x: x[1]-x[2], reverse=True)[:5]:
    print(f'  {n}: P3={a:.4f} SR={b:.4f}')
print(f'ScaledRamp worse on {len(worse_sr)} datasets:')
for n,a,b in sorted(worse_sr, key=lambda x: x[2]-x[1], reverse=True)[:5]:
    print(f'  {n}: P3={a:.4f} SR={b:.4f}')
" 2>/dev/null

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

datasets = load_all_datasets()

def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

# Show all datasets with nmse > 0.01 -- the improvable ones
results = []
for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    def res(c):
        p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
        if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
        return p - y_train
    r = least_squares(res, [0.5,1.0,0.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
    pv = pred_P3(X_val, r.x)
    nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
    results.append((ds_name, nm, r.x))

results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
print('Datasets with nmse > 0.01:')
for n,v,c in results:
    if v > 0.01:
        x1_unique = np.unique(datasets[[d[0] for d in datasets].index(n) if n in [d[0] for d in datasets] else 0][2][:,1]) if False else []
        print(f'  {n[:60]:60s} {v:.4f}  c2={c[2]:.3f} c3={c[3]:.3f} c6={c[6]:.3f}')
print(f'Sum of top-10 NMSE: {sum(r[1] for r in results[:10]):.4f}')
print(f'Sum of all NMSE: {sum(r[1] for r in results):.4f}')
print(f'Mean NMSE: {np.mean([r[1] for r in results]):.4f}')

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# Kakkar datasets: c3 is negative (-0.58 to -0.85), c2 near zero, c6 near zero
# c3*log(x1) with c3<0 means half-time DECREASES with x1 (higher conc -> faster)
# c6 (x1 power law rate) is near 0 -> rate is ~constant in x1
# The issue: c2 (tanh(x0) coefficient) is near zero -> secondary ramp inactive
# 
# Let's look at the actual Kakkar data shape more carefully

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kakkar2016_14kDa_Ex45_polyQ_sec' in ds_name:
        x0, x1 = X_train[:,0], X_train[:,1]
        print(f'Dataset: {ds_name}')
        # For x1=8 (most data), show the curve
        mask = x1 == 8.0
        x0c = x0[mask]; yc = y_train[mask]
        idx = np.argsort(x0c)
        x0s, ys = x0c[idx], yc[idx]
        print(f'x1=8 curve (first 15 points):')
        for i in range(0, min(15, len(x0s))):
            print(f'  t={x0s[i]:.0f}  y={ys[i]:.4f}')
        print(f'  ...')
        print(f'  t={x0s[-1]:.0f}  y={ys[-1]:.4f}')
        
        # What does P3 predict?
        def pred_P3(X, c):
            x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
            c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
            lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
            lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
            rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
            half = c3*lx1 + c5
            return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)
        
        def res(c):
            p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
            return p - y_train
        r = least_squares(res, [0.5,1.0,0.0,0.5,0.5,10.0,0.0], loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
        
        # Show residuals for x1=8
        pv_train = pred_P3(X_train[mask], r.x)
        print(f'P3 predictions vs actual (x1=8, first 15):')
        for i in range(0, min(15, len(x0s))):
            xi = np.array([[x0s[i], 8.0]])
            pi = pred_P3(xi, r.x)[0]
            print(f'  t={x0s[i]:.0f}  y={ys[i]:.4f}  pred={pi:.4f}  err={ys[i]-pi:.4f}')
        break

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from disease_relevant_noninhibited_all.evaluator import load_all_datasets
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares
import sympy as sp

datasets = load_all_datasets()

# The Kakkar data has replicate noise - the NMSE floor is set by within-replicate variance
# Let's compute the irreducible NMSE for Kakkar datasets

for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
    if 'Kakkar2016' in ds_name and '14kDa_Ex45_polyQ_sec' in ds_name:
        x0, x1 = X_train[:,0], X_train[:,1]
        # Compute within-group variance
        within_var = []
        for t in np.unique(x0):
            for conc in np.unique(x1):
                mask = (x0==t) & (x1==conc)
                if mask.sum() > 1:
                    within_var.append(np.var(y_train[mask]))
        total_var = np.var(y_train)
        irr = np.mean(within_var)/total_var if within_var else 0
        print(f'Kakkar14kDa_sec: total_var={total_var:.4f}, mean_within_var={np.mean(within_var):.4f}, irr_nmse={irr:.4f}')
        print(f'  Irreducible NMSE fraction: {irr:.4f}')
        break

# Now test: can a DIFFERENT initial value for c5 help the Kakkar datasets?
# The Kakkar half-times are ~6k-28k seconds, log(20000)~9.9
# Current c5_init=10.0 should be fine
# But c3_init=0.5 might be wrong -- Kakkar has c3~-0.85 (negative)
# Let's test with c3_init=-0.5

def pred_P3(X, c):
    x0, x1 = X[:,0], X[:,1]
    c0,c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6 = c
    lx0 = np.log(x0 + np.sqrt(x0**2 + 1.0))
    lx1 = np.log(np.maximum(x1, 1e-10))
    rate = c1 * np.maximum(x1, 1e-10)**c6
    half = c3*lx1 + c5
    return c0*np.tanh(rate*(lx0 - half)) + c4 + c2*np.tanh(x0)

# Test c3_init=-0.5 vs c3_init=0.5 across ALL datasets
for c3_init in [0.5, -0.5, 0.0]:
    init = [0.5, 1.0, 0.0, c3_init, 0.5, 10.0, 0.0]
    nmse_list = []
    worst = []
    for ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in datasets:
        def res(c):
            p = pred_P3(X_train, c)
            if not np.all(np.isfinite(p)): return np.full(len(y_train), 1e12)
            return p - y_train
        r = least_squares(res, init, loss='soft_l1', max_nfev=1000)
        pv = pred_P3(X_val, r.x)
        nm = np.mean((pv-y_val)**2)/(np.var(y_val)+1e-10)
        nmse_list.append(nm)
        if nm > 0.03:
            worst.append((ds_name[:35], nm))
    mean_nm = np.mean(nmse_list)
    print(f'c3_init={c3_init:+.1f}: mean_nmse={mean_nm:.4f} fit={1/(1+mean_nm):.4f}')
    worst.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    for n,v in worst[:3]:
        print(f'  {n}: {v:.4f}')
" 2>/dev/null
